                                                                  Technical Note

                                                      Additional information concerning the preparation
                                                      of the labor force, employment, hours and earnings,
                                                      and labor turnover series—concepts and scope,
                                                      survey methods, and limitations—is contained in
                                                      technical notes for each of these series, available
                                                      from the Bureau of Labor Statistics free of charge.



                   INTRODUCTION                                                        Relation between the household and payroll series

                   it were possible to take a complete census using the               magnitude of the standard errors rather than as the
                   same schedules and procedures.                                     precise standard error for any specific item.

                      Under cooperative arrangements with State agencies,                Form BLS 790 provides for entry of data on the num-
                   the respondent fills out only one employment or labor              ber of full- and part-time workers on the payrolls of non-
                   turnover schedule, which is then used for national, State,         agricultural establishments and, for most industries,
                   and area estimates. This eliminates duplicate reporting            payroll and man-hours of production and related workers
                   on the part of respondents and, together with the use of           or nonsupervisory workers for the pay period which
                   identical techniques at the national and State levels,             includes the 12th of the month. The labor turnover
                   insures maximum comparability of estimates.                        schedule provides for the collection of information on

                       State agencies mail the forms to the establishments            during the calendar month.
                   and examine the returns for consistency, accuracy, and
                   completeness. The States use the information to prepare
                   State and area series and then send the data to the BLS            CONCEPTS

                   Design
                                                                                      Coverage
                      The sampling plan used in the current employment
                  statistics program is an optimum allocation design known                The BLS sample of establishment employment and
                  as "sampling proportionate to average size of establish-            payrolls is the largest monthly sampling operation in the
                  ment." The universe of establishments is stratified first           field of social statistics. The table that follows shows
                  by industry and then within each industry by size of                the approximate proportion of total employment in each
                  establishment in terms of employment. For each industry             industry division covered by the group of establishments
                  the total size of the sample is distributed among the size          furnishing monthly employment data. The coverage for
                  class cells on the basis of average employment per                  individual industries within the division may vary from
                  establishment in each cell. In practice, this is equivalent         the proportions shown.
                  to distributing the predetermined total number of estab-
                  lishments required in the sample among the cells on the
                  basis of the ratio of employment in each cell to total                 Approximate size and coverage of BLS employment
                  employment in the industry. Within each stratum the                           and payrolls sample, March 1966 1
                  sample members are selected at random.

                                                                     ATTENTION

                         As discussed in the Technical Note, the Bureau periodically adjusts the industry employ-
                   ment series to a recent benchmark to improve their accuracy. These adjustments may also
                   affect the hours and earnings series because employment levels are used as weights. Industry
                   data for all national series shown in this report have been adjusted to March 1966 benchmarks.
                   Data from April 1966 forward are subject to revision at the time of the next benchmark.

                         Beginning with the September 1967 and subsequent issues of Employment and Earnings
                   and Monthly Report on the Labor Force, the national data in sections B, C, and D supersede
                   those published in previous issues, as well as those appearing in the Handbook of Labor Statis-
                   tics, 1967. Comparable data will be published in Employment and Earnings Statistics for the
                   United States, 1909-67, BLS Bulletin 1312-5.

                           Industry titles conform to the Bureau of the Budget's standard list of short SIC titles--
                   definitions are unchanged.
